title: 1.1: Reports HistoryBuf behavior under high output load with observed metrics (e.g., 10,000 lines pushed in ~0.04 seconds, final count values)
1.2: Identifies the segment allocation constant (e.g., SEGMENT_SIZE 2048 or similar fixed chunk size)
1.3: Describes the segment lookup mechanism (e.g., segment_for function, seg_num = y / SEGMENT_SIZE calculation)
1.4: Explains the pager history ring buffer mechanism (e.g., PagerHistoryBuf, pagerhist_extend, 1MB growth increments)
1.5: Reports the circular buffer wrap behavior (e.g., modular arithmetic with start_of_data, idx = (start_of_data + count) % ynum)
1.6: Identifies the buffer capacity threshold condition (e.g., count equals ynum, or count reaches buffer size)
1.7: Reports runtime test output showing buffer state (e.g., line content after wrap, count values, eviction evidence)
1.8: Explains logical-to-physical index translation (e.g., index_of formula, how line(0) maps to most recent entry)
1.9: Reports how evicted lines are preserved (e.g., ANSI serialization via pagerhist_push, ring buffer storage)
1.10: Identifies line continuation handling (e.g., is_continued attribute, next_char_was_wrapped flag, newline checking)
